authors: Chaupain-Guillot; Guillot
journal: The European Journal of Health Economics
data_source: EU-SILC
main_topic: Health-system characteristics, OOP, medical and dental unmet need
notes: Core EU-SILC microdata paper; OOP significant, provider density/access rules mostly not significant.

authors: Madureira-Lima; Reeves; Clair; Stuckler
data_source: EU-SILC pseudo-panel
main_topic: Great Recession, unemployment, OOP, income replacement, unmet medical need
notes: Strong motivation for unemployment, poverty/financial hardship, OOP, and social protection variables.

authors: Israel
journal: International Journal for Equity in Health
data_source: EU-SILC
main_topic: Financial accessibility, social protection, low-income groups, OOP, physician density
notes: Useful for social allowance/social protection and Q1/Q2 vulnerability framing.

authors: Ramos; Quintal; Lourenco; Antunes
data_source: European Social Survey
main_topic: Measurement decomposition of unmet need into need prevalence and unmet need among those in need
notes: Measurement citation; warns that whole-population unmet need mixes need burden and access conditional on need.

authors: Cylus; Papanicolas
data_source: European Social Survey
main_topic: Perceived access barriers under universal coverage, income gradients, OOP comparison
notes: Complements EU-SILC with perceived future access and non-price rationing discussion.

authors: Sheelah Connolly; Maev-Ann Wren
data_source: EU-SILC Ireland
main_topic: Unmet healthcare need, income, eligibility, insurance, cost and waiting reasons
notes: Single-country EU-SILC analysis showing cost dominates Irish unmet need but waiting-list barriers matter for public-card holders.

authors: Jon Ivar Elstad
journal: International Journal for Equity in Health
data_source: EU-SILC
main_topic: Great Recession, income inequality, GDP decline, foregone medical care
notes: Directly relevant multilevel EU-SILC panel evidence linking GDP decline and inequality to foregone care, especially among disadvantaged people.

authors: Monika Ewa Kaminska; Melike Wulfgramm
data_source: EU-SILC plus OECD, World Bank, WHO, HiT macro indicators
main_topic: Out-of-pocket payments and income-related unmet health need
notes: Key source for OOP share and income-gradient interaction; supports health-financing covariates in Mahdi's models.

authors: Giovanni Carnazza; Paolo Liberati; Giuliano Resce
data_source: European Health Interview Survey
main_topic: Income-related inequality in unmet care, medicines, waiting, and distance barriers
notes: Useful for reason-specific interpretation; separates affordability, waiting-list, and distance/transport mechanisms.

authors: Damiano Fiorillo
journal: International Journal of Health Economics and Management
data_source: EU-SILC
main_topic: Social capital, social support, and reasons for unmet healthcare needs
notes: Adds individual social-support mechanisms and reason-specific findings; less central to macro panel models but useful for limitations and omitted mechanisms.

authors: Valerie Moran; Ellen Nolte; Marc Suhrcke; Maria Ruiz-Castell
data_source: European Health Interview Survey plus Eurostat/OECD/World Bank
countries: 27 EU countries plus Iceland and Norway
main_topic: Unmet need reasons, utilisation, health-system characteristics, country-level access dimensions
notes: Very relevant recent EHIS paper separating waiting, distance, medical affordability, medicines affordability, GP use, and specialist use.

authors: Jens Detollenaere; Lise Hanssens; Veerle Vyncke; Jan De Maeseneer; Sara Willems
data_source: EU-SILC aggregate data plus PHAMEU
main_topic: Primary-care strength and income-related inequity in unmet need
notes: Macro-level evidence linking Gini, primary-care accessibility, and workforce development to the unmet-need income gap.

authors: Tae Jun Kim; Nico Vonneilich; Daniel Ludecke; Olaf von dem Knesebeck
data_source: International Social Survey Programme plus World Bank/OECD
main_topic: Income, financial barriers, public health expenditure, forgone care
notes: Useful financing and inequality evidence, but less Europe-specific; public expenditure share did not moderate income-gradient in forgone care.

authors: Aaron Reeves; Martin McKee; Johan Mackenbach; Margaret Whitehead; David Stuckler
journal: Journal of Epidemiology and Community Health
data_source: Eurostat EU-SILC plus Comparative Welfare Entitlements Dataset
main_topic: Public pensions, OOP payments, older people, cost-related unmet medical need
notes: Strong panel-policy source showing social protection can buffer cost-related unmet need, especially under high OOP exposure.

authors: Patrick Doupe; James Faghmous; Sanjay Basu
data_source: Methodology primer with simulated claims data
main_topic: Machine learning methods for health services research
notes: Useful methods citation for regularisation, validation, overfitting, tree ensembles, neural networks, and prediction-versus-causation framing.

authors: Shiho Kino; Yu-Tien Hsu; Koichiro Shiba; Yung-Shin Chien; Carol Mita; Ichiro Kawachi; Adel Daoud
data_source: Scoping review of ML and social determinants of health
main_topic: Machine learning for social determinants, prediction, fairness, causal support, data curation
notes: Methods/background citation for applying ML to social determinants and inequality while keeping causal claims limited.

authors: Jason Denzil Morgenstern; Emmalin Buajitti; Meghan O'Neill; Thomas Piggott; Vivek Goel; Daniel Fridman; Kathy Kornas; Laura C. Rosella
data_source: Scoping review of ML prediction studies in population health
countries: International; most common United States and China
main_topic: Population-health prediction, validation, reporting, calibration
notes: Strong methods citation for validation, reporting, generalisability, calibration, and comparison with simpler baselines.

authors: Woojung Lee; Naomi Schwartz; Aasthaa Bansal; Sara Khor; Noah Hammarlund; Anirban Basu; Beth Devine
data_source: Scoping review of ML in HEOR using nonwearable data
main_topic: Machine learning in health economics and outcomes research
notes: Methods citation showing tree-based models are common and economic/resource-use outcomes are underexplored.

authors: Alok Ranjan; Sundararaman Thiagarajan; Govindaraj Shanmugam; Samir Garg
data_source: Household survey with perceived and latent unmet healthcare need measures
years: Cross-sectional survey; year not clearly stated in extracted text
main_topic: Measurement of unmet healthcare need, latent need, incomplete care, inappropriate care
notes: Useful for conceptual and limitations sections because self-reported unmet need can miss latent and unrecognised need.

filename: Rare disease care in Europe – Gaping unmet needs.pdf
authors: Philippe Pakter
data_source: Legal and policy review of rare disease access in Europe
main_topic: Rare disease treatment access, orphan medicines, reimbursement, cross-border healthcare, Member-State inequality
notes: Background example of effective-access inequality across European health systems; less central because not EU-SILC or population-level unmet medical examination.

filename: Access to healthcare for people aged 50+ in Europe.pdf
authors: Sime Smolic; Ivan Cipin; Petra Medimurec
data_source: SHARE Corona Survey plus SHARE Wave 7 and country-level COVID/system indicators
main_topic: COVID-era forgone, postponed, and denied care among adults aged 50+
notes: Relevant micro-macro evidence on pandemic access disruptions, economic strain, poor health, country variation, UHC, and containment measures.

authors: Dimitris Zavras; Athanasios I. Zavras; Ilias-Ioannis Kyriopoulos; John Kyriopoulos
data_source: EU-SILC plus Greek national health surveys
main_topic: Economic crisis, austerity, unemployment, income, insurance, and financial unmet need
notes: Direct EU-SILC crisis/austerity source supporting unemployment, income, financial barriers, and insurance/social protection covariates.

filename: Economic vulnerability and unmet healthcare needs.pdf
authors: Louis Arnault; Florence Jusot; Thomas Renaud
data_source: SHARE Wave 8 plus SHARE Corona Survey
main_topic: Economic vulnerability and COVID-era unmet healthcare among adults aged 50+
notes: Strong inequality source showing financial strain, poor health, country heterogeneity, and interaction effects across unmet-need types.

filename: Effects of the financial crisis and Troika austerity measures.pdf
authors: Helena Legido-Quigley; Marina Karanikolos; Sonia Hernandez-Plaza; Claudia de Freitas; Luis Bernardo; Beatriz Padilla; Rita Sa Machado; Karla Diaz-Ordaz; David Stuckler; Martin McKee
data_source: EU-SILC plus OECD/Eurostat/Portuguese Ministry and policy evidence
main_topic: Troika austerity, co-payments/OOP financing, and unmet medical need
notes: Direct EU-SILC Portugal source linking austerity, financial barriers, waiting times, public/private financing, and OOP exposure.

authors: Valerie Moran; Marc Suhrcke; Maria Ruiz-Castell; Jessica Barre; Laetitia Huiart
data_source: European Health Interview Survey wave 2
main_topic: Reason-specific unmet need: waits, distance, medical/dental/medicines/mental-health affordability
notes: Useful EHIS micro evidence for measurement, reason-specific barriers, income gradients, social support, and high-resource-system access gaps.

authors: Marina Karanikolos; Vladimir S. Gordeev; Johan P. Mackenbach; Martin McKee
data_source: EU-SILC cross-sectional microdata
main_topic: Financial crisis, austerity, affordability, waiting lists, and unmet medical need
notes: Direct EU-SILC crisis paper showing different Baltic policy responses produced cost barriers in Latvia and waiting-list barriers in Estonia.

authors: Ivan Maslyankov
data_source: Systematic review of quantitative studies using self-reported unmet healthcare need
countries: Albania; Bosnia and Herzegovina; Bulgaria; Greece; Kosovo; Montenegro; Serbia; North Macedonia; Romania
main_topic: Southeastern Europe unmet healthcare needs evidence, cost barriers, socioeconomic disadvantage
notes: Regional synthesis useful for Balkan/Southeastern Europe motivation; highlights cost/OOP barriers, disadvantaged groups, and uneven evidence quality.

authors: Aida Isabel Tavares
data_source: SHARE COVID-19 Survey plus SHARE Wave 7 and Eurostat/Oxford macro indicators
main_topic: COVID first-wave unmet care among adults aged 50+, OOP, beds, doctors, nurses, system type
notes: Relevant micro-macro SHARE paper linking unmet care to economic strain, poor health, high OOP, Beveridge systems, and health-system resources.

filename: Predictors of unmet health care needs in.pdf
authors: Natasa Popovic; Zorica Terzic-Supic; Snezana Simic; Biljana Mladenovic
data_source: Serbian SILC / EU-SILC methodology
main_topic: Predictors and reasons for unmet healthcare need in Serbia
notes: Direct EU-SILC-family country study; supports income, health status, region, education, financial barriers, waiting lists, and access reason categories.

filename: Predictors of Unmet Healthcare Needs during Economic and.pdf
authors: George Pierrakos; Aspasia Goula; Dimitra Latsou
journal: International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health
data_source: Eurostat EU-SILC and macro indicators
main_topic: Eurostat time-series predictors of unmet medical need during economic and health crises
notes: Close to Mahdi's Eurostat macro design; supports unemployment, GDP, public/private expenditure, income gradients, health status, and non-causal interpretation.

filename: Unmet Health Care Needs of the Older Population in European.pdf
authors: Ewa Kocot
data_source: Eurostat EHIS unmet-healthcare-need indicators
countries: 26 EU countries plus Iceland and Norway
main_topic: Older population unmet healthcare needs by age, reason, income, education, urbanization, activity limitation
notes: Useful Eurostat measurement paper comparing older and younger groups; highlights EHIS versus EU-SILC denominator differences and age-specific reason patterns.

filename: Unmet healthcare needs among older Europeans trends,.pdf
authors: Athanasios Chantzaras; John Yfantopoulos
data_source: SHARE wave 8 plus Eurostat public health expenditure; descriptive comparison with SHARE wave 1
main_topic: Older Europeans unmet needs, public health expenditure, income, specialist access, service-specific unmet need
notes: Strong financing/capacity citation linking public expenditure to lower unmet needs; useful for macro predictors, country heterogeneity, and non-causal interpretation.
